steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to explain an advantage of the Platanares binary cycle system using Fazal's reasoning. The correct choice must both name a benefit specific to that system and include Fazal's causal logic for why it works.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Compared with dry steam systems and flash steam systems, the binary cycle system used at the Platanares plant is "capable of producing more energy per megawatt per year," Fazal notes.": Misattributes Fazal's comparison—she describes the Platanares system's own benefit, not a ranking against other systems. ✗
  • The high-temperature-fluid alternative uses high-temperature geothermal fluid to produce electricity, the system used at the Platanares plant results in "little or no emissions," making it environmentally friendly.": Cites a step in the process but does not frame it as an advantage or include Fazal's reasoning for why it matters. ✗
  • "Since they return all geothermal fluid to the injection well, binary cycle systems—such as the one at the Platanares plant—produce "the least environmental impact," according to Fazal.": Uses Fazal's exact quoted advantage and preserves her causal logic (returning 100% of geothermal fluid produces minimal emissions), directly accomplishing the goal. ✓
  • The remaining alternative uses a binary cycle system to produce electricity; in this type of geothermal system, fluid is pumped "back to the injection well," Fazal explains.": Alters Fazal's reasoning in a way that breaks the connection between the fluid-return mechanism and the environmental benefit she describes. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
